steps.title: Identify what the function s(t) represents
steps.content:
The problem states that $s(t)$ gives the approximate mass of this isotope that remains after $t$ years.

Critically, this function outputs the amount still present, not the amount that has decayed.

This distinction immediately eliminates choices B and C, which misinterpret the function output.

steps.title: Decode the equation s(58) = 32
steps.content:
The equation $s(58) = 32$ tells us:
  • Input: $t = 58$ years have elapsed
  • Output: $s(58) = 32$ grams remain
Since $s(t)$ represents remaining mass, this equation directly means that 32 grams of the sample remain 58 years after decay begins. That matches choice A — A is correct.
